properties: Trachoma societatis, also known as desert marigold or Baileya multiradiata, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may contribute to the plant's potential therapeutic uses, such as soothing skin irritations and reducing inflammation. In some cultures, the plant has been used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and colds. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic properties and potential applications.
